laws.title: Georgia Restrictive Covenants Act — Non-Compete Enforceability
laws.summary: Georgia's Restrictive Covenants Act (O.C.G.A. § 13-8-50 et seq.) provides one of the most employer-friendly non-compete frameworks in the country. Covenants are enforceable if reasonable in time (up to 2 years), territory, and scope. Courts may blue-pencil overbroad restrictions. Georgia law applies to agreements signed after May 11, 2011. Non-solicitation and confidentiality agreements are separately enforceable.
compliance_actions: Review all non-compete, non-solicitation, and NDA templates for Georgia-specific compliance
Ensure covenants specify a clear time limit (≤2 years), defined territory, and scope
Include a choice-of-Georgia-law clause to maximize enforceability
Be aware that federal FTC non-compete rule is currently blocked — rely on Georgia law

laws.title: Georgia Preemption of Local Employment Ordinances
laws.summary: Georgia state law preempts local governments from enacting minimum wage, paid leave, or other employment benefit mandates that exceed state law. This means unlike California or New York, Georgia employers face a single statewide set of rules — there are no Atlanta-specific wage or leave laws to track separately. Georgia follows federal minimum wage ($7.25/hr) since the state has not enacted a higher rate.
compliance_actions: Georgia follows federal minimum wage — no state or local premium applies
No Georgia-specific paid sick leave requirement — federal FMLA applies
Confirm multi-state employees' home state for leave and wage purposes
Monitor Georgia legislature for any future changes to this preemption

laws.title: Georgia Drug-Free Workplace — Workers' Comp Premium Discount
laws.summary: Employers certified as a Georgia Drug-Free Workplace receive a 7.5% workers' compensation premium discount. Certification requires a written policy, supervisor training, employee education, access to EAP services, and drug/alcohol testing programs. Testing must follow specific protocols — both pre-employment and post-accident testing are recommended.
compliance_actions: Confirm your drug-free workplace policy is current and distributed to all employees
Ensure supervisors receive required annual training on drug/alcohol impairment
Maintain testing program covering pre-employment, reasonable suspicion, and post-accident
Apply for and maintain Georgia Drug-Free Workplace certification for the workers' comp discount
